kept.text:
GM web3
Happy new week 

Did you know?
How the Sharing Economy is evolving

And how @ShareX_Network is changing how people access and earn from real world resources. 

Most people already use the sharing economy without realizing it.
Think Uber or Airbnb.
You don’t own the car or house you just use it when needed.

The idea is simple:
👉 Access > Ownership
Instead of buying everything, you just pay to use it.

But there’s a problem…
These platforms are centralized
They control users, data, and take a big cut.

Now comes Web3 👇
What if users owned the system instead of a company?
Direct payments
More transparency
Less middlemen

This leads to something powerful: DePIN
(Decentralized Physical Infrastructure Networks)

DePIN is people sharing real-world devices
Internet
Energy
Storage
Power banks
…and earning from them

This connects to RWA (Real World Assets)
Turning physical things into income-generating assets

Example:
Instead of just owning a device…
👉 You deploy it
👉 People use it
👉 You earn from it

So the evolution looks like this:
Web2: Platform owns everything
Web3: Users own the network

In simple terms:
👉 Sharing economy = use instead of own
👉 Web3 = own what you use
👉 DePIN = earn from real-world usage

This is bigger than crypto.
It’s about changing how people access and earn from real-world resources.

profile_bio.description: Bridging Web2↔Web3 @ShareX_Network |Building connections in the crypto revolution| Astrophile|mufc🔴

quoted_tweet.text:
We booked a Stayvista property in Munnar from Airbnb for ₹36k. 

From the start, there were many issues: leeches, cockroaches, a non-working gas stove, fungus on curtains, damaged non-stick utensils in the kitchen, and flickering lights. It has been the worst experience. We booked for 2 days but are leaving today.

When we contacted Airbnb support, they said we lacked enough evidence of a bad experience, would follow whatever the host says, and offered only a ₹1500 refund.

Worst experience with Airbnb customer support. If you book from Airbnb, don’t expect any support if things go wrong.

don’t ever book this property in Munnar. The house is full of leeches. Avoid Stayvista or Airbnb from now on. 😞😞😞
@AirbnbHelp @Airbnb_in @Airbnb @bchesky @amanpreet_bajaj

kept.text:
My friend flew back home last Christmas after 3 years abroad. First time back. Everyone was excited.
​She stayed in an Airbnb.
​Her mother called every day asking why she wouldn’t sleep at home. She kept saying it was "more convenient."
​But one evening she told me the truth: "I love them. But I’ve heard too many stories. People go back fine and return different or even fail to return. I’m not taking chances."
​I didn't know what else to tell her . The scary part is I understood her completely. 😶

kept.text:
How Airbnb was actually created?

Brian and Joe were living in San Francisco, almost broke and struggling to pay rent.

Right when things were getting tight, a big conference hit the city and every hotel was fully booked.

So they did something simple:
They put 3 air mattresses in their apartment and rented them out for $80/night.
They called it “Air Bed and Breakfast.”

In one week, they made $1,000.
Rent covered.

That’s when it clicked this could be bigger.
They built a simple website and started pitching investors.
Most of them laughed.
“No one’s going to let strangers stay in their homes.”

But one investor gave them $20.000  and said:
“Go knock on doors.”

So they went to New York.
Met hosts in person.
Took professional photos of their homes.
Then the flywheel started spinning:
More bookings → more trust
More listings → more supply
More supply → more demand

Without realizing it, they did one thing perfectly:
They didn’t wait for perfection.
They launched with what they had. (MVP mindset)

What started with 3 air mattresses…
turned into a platform with millions of listings.
Sometimes big ideas don’t start with changing the world they start with just trying to make rent.

kept.text:
O quarto de hóspedes, que na real era um quadro de bagunça e malas, virou um estúdio/escritorio. Ainda falta pintar uma das paredes de verde, pra fazer live de VR. 

E colocar mais estantes com plantas, pra dar um aspecto mais gostosinho. 

E mais alguns quadros. E instalar um estofado na parede perto da cama, porque fica mais c uma função de sofá. Esse espelho atrás da mesa de gravação eu quero substituir por um espelho bem maior

E finalmente uma tela retrátil na frente da parede verde, pra assistir filme sentado na cama/sofá. O que significa um projeto acima da cama. 

Entre uma aula e outra eu deito nesse sofá, leio, escrevo no diário, toco guitarra, ou brinco c os ratinhos. A gaiola tá do lado da cama por causa disso 

Tô deixando o apê daorinha pq no período em que for pro Canadá, a intenção é usá-lo como AirBnB
